chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss

text: 3926 - وَ رَوَى عُثْمَانُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ جَمَّالاً لَنَا كَانَ يُكَارِينَا فَحَمَلَ عَلَى غَيْرِهِ فَضَاعَ قَالَ "ضَمِّنْهُ وَ خُذْ مِنْهُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3926 - Uthman bin Ziyad narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
I said to Imam (as): "We had a camel driver who used to work for us. He loaded goods onto someone else's camel, and they were lost."
Imam (as) said: "Hold him liable and take compensation from him."
